subject: Re: Incorrect tomogram positioning for reconstructions in Warp
body:
Hi, Warp does not take into account the positioning of the tomogram. Within
the IMOD workflow, you should use the outputs from the fine alignments step
and skip the tomogram position step. In the AreTomo workflow do not correct
for the pre-tilt.

On Sat, 8 Oct 2022 at 05:12, R Nwo <rnwokonko@gmail.com> wrote:

> I am trying to reconstruct some tomograms from FIB'd lamellae in Warp
> using .xf alignment files generated by AreTomo/IMOD. My aligned tilts/tomos
> turn out fine in AreTomo/IMOD, but my reconstructions from Warp appear
> devoid of any cellular information as if they are choosing an incorrect
> center for the reconstruction (either above or below the true center). For
> some tomograms with ice contamination, you can just start to see the faint
> appearance of crystals as you reach the end of the tomogram, which is what
> lead me to the conclusion that the reconstruction center is off.
>
> Is there a way to fix the tomogram positioning, similar to what is done in
> IMOD? Additionally, am I missing some additional information besides the
> .xf file to help Warp identify the proper thickness/depth to center the
> reconstruction?
>
> Thanks,
>
> Robert
>
> --
